page: Microsoft Azure NC288lds_xl_RTXPRO6000BSE_v6 is a virtualized server instance running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 288 dedicated Intel vCPUs. The hardware configuration includes 516.0 GB of system memory, which translates to a low memory allocation of 1.79 GB per core. Additionally, the instance is equipped with a single GPU and a large local storage capacity of 15841 GB, supporting Premium Storage.

The primary tradeoff of this instance lies in its resource density. While it offers massive parallel compute capability with 288 dedicated vCPUs and substantial local storage, the memory-to-core ratio is constrained. This low-memory profile makes it less suitable for memory-bound workloads but highly cost-efficient for compute-heavy tasks that require local scratch space and GPU acceleration without requiring massive RAM footprints per thread.

Given these specifications, the instance is designed for workloads such as GPU-accelerated parallel processing, large-scale local data processing, rendering, and compute-heavy simulations that benefit from high vCPU counts and local storage but do not demand high memory-per-core ratios.
description: Microsoft Azure NC288lds_xl_RTXPRO6000BSE_v6 is an x86_64 virtual server powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 288 dedicated Intel vCPUs, 516.0 GB of system memory, one GPU, and 15841 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. A key architectural tradeoff is its low memory-to-core ratio of 1.79 GB per vCPU, positioning it as a specialized compute-heavy instance. This resource profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for parallel processing tasks that require high CPU cores and local storage but have modest memory requirements per thread. Appropriate workloads include GPU-accelerated simulations, large-scale rendering, and local data processing tasks.
